summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, AI / privacy, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are ‘If he didn’t do it, who did?’: fresh doubts over 2007 murder case that gripped Italy, ‘Stuck in the mud’: one year on, Friedrich Merz struggles to find his footing, and Apple agrees to pay iPhone owners $250 million for not delivering AI Siri.

mainItems.title: ‘If he didn’t do it, who did?’: fresh doubts over 2007 murder case that gripped Italy
mainItems.summary: Fresh DNA evidence in the killing of Chiara Poggi has brought a previously investigated suspect back under scrutiny, raising questions over the conviction A suspect in a case that has captivated Italy for almost 19 years has been summoned…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: ‘Stuck in the mud’: one year on, Friedrich Merz struggles to find his footing
mainItems.summary: Just 19% of Germans approve of the chancellor’s leadership – with some of his comments causing offence amid spats with the White House Friedrich Merz’s first steps as German chancellor proved to be a stumble and, one year on, he is still…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Apple agrees to pay iPhone owners $250 million for not delivering AI Siri
mainItems.summary: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit that accused it of misleading customers about the availability of its Apple Intelligence features.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over claims chatbot posed as doctor
mainItems.summary: State officials allege a Character.AI bot claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist and provided a fake state medical license number.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Character.AI sued over chatbot that claims to be a real doctor with a license
mainItems.summary: State says chatbot claimed to practice medicine, gave invalid license number.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Apple could let you pick a favorite AI model in iOS 27
mainItems.summary: The next update to Apple's operating systems could allow users to choose their preferred AI model for running Apple Intelligence.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Los Angeles Unified Teachers to Provide High-Dosage Tutoring
mainItems.summary: The Los Angeles Unified School District is looking to focus on teacher-led, high-dosage tutoring to meet the requirements of a settlement that requires LAUSD to provide 10 million hours of tutoring to 100,000 students over three years.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: US to take steps to curb antidepressant prescribing, RFK Jr says
mainItems.summary: Plan promotes ‘deprescribing’ as psychiatrists warn crisis stems from lack of access to care, not overuse The federal health department will begin a series of steps intended to curb antidepressant use in the US, Robert F Kennedy Jr…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Scott Turow's latest real-life legal thriller: Suing Meta for copyright infringement
mainItems.summary: Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: Dead as Disco is out in early access to fill the Hi-Fi Rush and, weirdly, Batman: Arkham series shaped hole in your heart
mainItems.summary: At this point in time, even if Tango Gameworks were ultimately saved , there's no telling when we might get a new Hi-Fi Rush game.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: NYC Parents and Students Demand Moratorium on AI Use at Marathon Meeting
mainItems.summary: Despite New York City’s last-minute withdrawal of a controversial proposal for a new artificial intelligence-centered high school, parents, students, and educators packed this week’s school board meeting to speak on AI anyway.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: New Trailer for Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey Looks Only Moderately Epic
mainItems.summary: There's no place like ... you know the rest The post New Trailer for Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey Looks Only Moderately Epic appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: Cruise ship with hantavirus may have seen a rare occurrence: humans infecting humans
mainItems.summary: Hantavirus is typically spread through contact with rodent feces, urine or saliva. But cases on the MV Hondius cruise ship point to an unusual means of transmission.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: Solve a murder mystery by flying people around on your giant crane in the Disco Elysium and Chinese folktale inspired The Crane Rider's Tale
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing at James's request.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
